---
title: "FirstPodsDeveloping"
format: html
---

The **FirstPodsDeveloping** phase in [Lentil](/docs/Plants/Lentil) represents the developmental period from **FirstFlowersSet** to **FirstPodsVisiable**. This phase covers early pod development following successful flower set.

## Overview

The [GenericPhase](/docs/Models/PMF/Phenology/Phases/GenericPhase.qmd) model simulates the pod development using daily [Thermal Time](ThermalTime.qmd).

- This phase starts at **FirstFlowersSet** and ends at **FirstPodsVisiable**
- The model uses daily thermal time, retrieved from the `[Phenology].ThermalTime` function
- Pod size increases and becomes visible
- Pollination and fertilization have occurred
- Pod wall development proceeds to make structures visible

## Pod Development Characteristics

During this phase:

- Successful flowers transition to developing pods
- Pod wall expands and becomes visible
- Seed number in developing pods is established
- Continued flowering occurs at upper nodes
- Lower pods are larger and more advanced than upper pods

## Cultivar-Specific Parameters

Cultivar-specific parameters that influence pod development:

| Name       | Description                                         | Default Value |
|------------|-----------------------------------------------------|---------------|
| [Phenology].MinFloralNodes | Minimum number of nodes bearing flowers  | 8 |
| [Phenology].MaxFloralNodes | Maximum number of nodes bearing flowers  | 15 |

## Practical Considerations

Factors affecting pod development:

1. **Temperature**: Controls rate of pod expansion and development
2. **Water availability**: Insufficient water leads to pod abortion and reduced set
3. **Radiation**: Photosynthetically active radiation supports pod development
4. **Nutrient status**: Nitrogen availability affects pod setting and development

The model simulates pod development as a temperature-dependent process, with environmental stress mechanisms affecting pod retention and viability.
